message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Passing tests: 29
Recent Green phase: Added `countsByType(entries, typeKey)` helper plus `assertDamageCountsWithinPolicy(damages, policyItems)` that throws "more damage entries for X than policy covers" when damage count exceeds insured count. Skip the check when policy covers zero of a type to defer that case to findPolicyItem.

Note: The only remaining todo is `it.todo("first quote in scenario adds 10% first-insurance surcharge (covered by earlier tests)")` — that's intentionally left as a marker.

Refactor the implementation while keeping all tests green.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return when done.

attachment.content:
- red: TDD Red Phase
- green: TDD Green Phase
- test-list: TDD Test List Phase
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
All 29 tests pass. Now let me evaluate refactoring opportunities.

## Naming Evaluation (FIRST PRIORITY)

Going through each function name:
- `runScenario` — clear, drives the steps
- `quote` — describes quote calculation
- `claim` — describes claim payout calculation
- `premiumEntries` — clear: returns base + surcharge for each entry
- `policyModifierPct` — clear: returns the policy-level percentage adjustment
- `itemSurchargePct` — clear
- `singleItemEntry` / `componentGroupEntry` — clear
- `componentBasePremium` — clear
- `previousQuoteCount` — clear
- `isHighEnchantmentClaim` — clear
- `reimbursableAmount` — clear
- `damagePayout` — clear
- `findPolicyItem` — clear
- `assertNonNegativeDamage` — clear
- `damageEntryPayout` — clear
- `countsByType` — clear (just added)
- `assertDamageCountsWithinPolicy` — clear (just added)
- `assertKnownItemTypes` — clear

All function names appear to reveal intent well. No naming improvements needed.

## APP Mass Evaluation - Looking for Refactoring Opportunities

Let me examine `countsByType` (just added). It uses object spread which is expensive. Also let me look at `assertDamageCountsWithinPolicy` and the component counting logic in `premiumEntries`:

## Identified Refactoring Opportunity (Rule 3 - Duplication)

I see clear duplication: `premiumEntries` reduces items into a count map by `item.type`, and `countsByType` does the exact same operation generically. This is conceptual duplication (Rule 3).

The component-counting logic in `premiumEntries`:
```typescript
.reduce<Record<string, number>>(
  (counts, item) => ({ ...counts, [item.type]: (counts[item.type] ?? 0) + 1 }),
  {},
)
```

is essentially `countsByType(componentItems, "type")`. Let me refactor to remove this duplication.

## Initial APP Mass (premiumEntries function)

Current `premiumEntries`:
- Constants: 1 (`{}`) 
- Bindings: ~10 (items, singles, item params, componentCounts, counts, componentGroups, type, count)
- Invocations: ~10 (assertKnownItemTypes, filter, has, map, singleItemEntry, filter, has, reduce, ?? , Object.entries, map, componentGroupEntry, spread)
- Conditionals: 0 (only `??` arguably)
- Loops: 4 (filter, map, filter, reduce, map = 5 loops × 5 = 25)
- Assignments: 0

Detailed mass for the duplicated reduce block: ~ Constants:1 + Bindings:3 + Invocations:3 + Loops:5 = 12

Let me apply the refactoring:

content.text: No build script, but tests pass. Let me verify the final state of the file:

## Refactor Phase Complete

### Refactoring Applied: Removed Duplication (Rule 3)

**Change**: Replaced an inline `reduce` block in `premiumEntries` (which counted items by `item.type`) with a call to the existing `countsByType(componentItems, "type")` helper. Moved `countsByType` above `premiumEntries` so it's defined before use.

### Naming Evaluation (First Priority)
All function names were evaluated against their current behavior — every name accurately reveals intent. No renames performed.

### APP Mass Comparison (premiumEntries function)

**Before**:
- Constants: 1 (`{}`)
- Bindings: 8 (items, singles, item×2, componentCounts, counts, item, componentGroups, type, count)
- Invocations: ~13 (assertKnownItemTypes, filter, !has, map, filter, has, reduce, spread, ??, Object.entries, map, componentGroupEntry, spread×2)
- Loops: 5 (filter, map, filter, reduce, map)
- Assignments: 0
- Approx mass: 1 + 8 + 26 + 25 = **~60**

**After**:
- Constants: 0
- Bindings: 7 (items, singles, item×2, componentItems, componentGroups, type, count)
- Invocations: ~10 (assertKnownItemTypes, filter, !has, map, filter, has, Object.entries, countsByType, map, componentGroupEntry, spread×2)
- Loops: 4 (filter, map, filter, map) — the reduce inside `countsByType` is still counted there, not here
- Assignments: 0
- Approx mass: 0 + 7 + 20 + 20 = **~47** (within `premiumEntries`)

### Rules Applied
- Rule 1 (Tests Pass): All 29 tests still pass
- Rule 2 (Reveals Intent): Intent slightly improved — the named helper `countsByType` makes the grouping operation more declarative than an inline `reduce` with spread
- Rule 3 (No Duplication): Eliminated the duplicate counting logic — there is now one canonical implementation
- Rule 4 (Fewest Elements): Reduced inline complexity in `premiumEntries`

### Test Status
- 29 passing tests
- 1 intentional `it.todo` marker (per Green-phase context)

### Files Modified
- `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_23-36-49_claim-office-lite-example-mapping_v6-hybrid_opus-4-7-portkey-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts`
